targetSenders().details: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

test_security_forceTransferBypassesFreez.details: SC-008b: Verify force transfer bypasses freeze

test_security_forceTransferOnlyComplianc.details: SC-008: Verify force transfer flag cannot be abused

test_security_frozenCannotTransfer().details: SC-007b: Verify frozen address cannot transfer

test_security_nonMemberBankSettlement_re.details: SC-009c: Non-member bank cannot receive settlement

test_security_onlyAdminCanGrantRoles().details: SC-003b: Verify only admin can grant roles NOTE: In OpenZeppelin AccessControl, each role can define who can grant it MINTER_ROLE has DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE as its admin role

test_security_operatorCannotEscalateToAd.details: SC-011: Operator cannot escalate to admin role

test_security_operatorCannotSetOperator(.details: SC-013: Operator cannot set another operator (only admin can)

test_security_operatorRolesRevokedOnChan.details: SC-012: Old operator truly loses mint/burn capabilities when operator changes

test_security_oracleManipulation_blocked.details: SC-006: Verify reserve oracle cannot be manipulated by non-attestor

test_security_overflowProtection().details: SC-010b: Test overflow protection in canMint NOTE: Solidity 0.8.x has built-in overflow checks that will revert

test_security_reentrancy_mint_protected(.details: SC-001: Verify reentrancy protection on mint

test_security_reentrancy_settlement_prot.details: SC-002: Verify reentrancy protection on settlement operations

test_security_roleEscalation_blocked().details: SC-003: Verify DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE cannot be self-assigned by non-admin NOTE: In OpenZeppelin AccessControl, only DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE can grant admin role This test verifies that a random address cannot grant itself admin

test_security_settlementCannotExecuteAft.details: SC-009b: Settlement cannot be executed after expiry

test_security_settlementExpiry().details: SC-009: Test settlement expiry mechanism NOTE: settlementMint checks reserve backing even for expired settlements This is intentional - GENIUS Act requires all tokens to be backed

test_security_settlementExpiry_succeedsW.details: SC-009d: Settlement expiry succeeds even with low reserves (FINDING-001 FIX) settlementReturn bypasses reserve check because tokens were already burned for the settlement This prevents funds from being locked if reserves decrease during settlement window

test_security_staleAttestation_blocksMin.details: SC-006c: Verify stale attestation blocks minting

test_security_whitelistBypass_blocked().details: SC-007: Verify whitelist bypass is not possible via _update
